title: Criminal Fines for Child Offenders
description: CBO estimates that broadening criminal fines and prison sentences for crimes against minors could increase the number of people incarcerated. The proposed law, S. 1002, would have no significant cost to the federal government but may affect direct spending and revenues. A later bill, H.R. 1842, would further amend federal criminal law to broaden the scope of certain fines and sentences.
